title: Skip layer digests for podman system check --quick
body:
In some cases, it is useful to opt for a quicker check if we prioritize detecting and fixing severe corruption and can tolerate minor damage.

The check option is derived from CRI-O's internal repair: https://github.com/cri-o/cri-o/blob/9e4d86d82370ad44c14649f28ebdd8f94aaa28ca/internal/lib/container_server.go#L860

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
`podman system check --quick` now also skips checking layer digests
```

comments.body: [NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

could we just skip digests checks with `--quick` and not have to expose a new knob?

Calculating the digest is expensive so it falls in the documented category of: "Skip checks which are known to be time-consuming." we have for `--quick`

> could we just skip digests checks with `--quick` and not have to expose a new knob?
> 
> Calculating the digest is expensive so it falls in the documented category of: "Skip checks which are known to be time-consuming." we have for `--quick`

+1 

It sounds like the risk profile will remain the same as well i.e "This will prevent some types of errors from being detected."
there is also a convention for things like verbosity if we cannot agree.  certainly seen things like `-v` and `-vv`

Thanks, all for the comments! I understand the concern, so I pick a suggestion that I think also makes sense, which is simply change the `--quick` to also skip layer digests, because in practice the current `--quick` does not feel quick enough, so I think having just one knob `--quick` is a good idea, and if this is missing too much detection, users can always opt for without the flag.

What do you all think?

> Once could argue switching which checks are performed could count as breaking change. The docs don't mention anything specifically so I think it is fine to switch this but maybe it would be best to make this clear in the docs, i.e. add a sentence like `The exact checks performed by this option are subject to change.`

Makes sense, added it to the doc.
